claimText: A loan is classified as a Non-Performing Asset (NPA) when the principal or interest remains overdue for more than 90 days.
evidenceExcerpt: A loan becomes an NPA when principal or interest remains overdue for more than 90 days.
examUse: Statement-based question on NPA classification criteria.

claimText: The SARFAESI Act mandates a 60-day demand notice to borrowers before financial institutions can initiate asset recovery actions.
evidenceExcerpt: The Act mandates a 60-day demand notice to borrowers before initiating asset recovery actions.
examUse: Statement-based question on SARFAESI Act procedures.
distractorHooks: No notice required for willful defaulters

claimText: Asset Reconstruction Companies (ARCs) are registered and regulated by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).
evidenceExcerpt: Asset Reconstruction Companies (ARCs) are registered and regulated by the RBI to buy NPAs and clean up bank balance sheets.
examUse: Institution-feature question on the regulatory authority of ARCs.
distractorHooks: Regulated by the Ministry of Finance
Regulated by Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI)

claimText: Under the SARFAESI framework, a contradiction exists regarding redemption rights: Section 13(8) states borrowers lose the right to redeem a secured asset once an auction notice is published, whereas Rules 8 & 9 allow redemption up until the auction date.
evidenceExcerpt: Contradiction exists between SARFAESI Act Section 13(8) (borrower loses redemption right upon auction notice publication) and Rules 8 & 9 (redemption allowed until auction date).
examUse: Statement-based question on borrower rights and legal provisions under the SARFAESI Act.
distractorHooks: Borrowers lose redemption rights immediately upon NPA classification
Rules 8 & 9 revoke redemption rights upon notice publication
